players.name: Bart Verbruggen
players.bio: At just 23, Verbruggen is one of Europe's most exciting young keepers. The Brighton stopper earned his Oranje spot with commanding displays and sharp reflexes. His first World Cup — and he's barely old enough to rent a car.
players.facts: Born in 2002, Verbruggen is younger than several FIFA video game editions featuring him.

players.name: Lutsharel Geertruida
players.bio: Versatile and composed, Geertruida can play right-back or centre-back with equal authority. A product of the Feyenoord academy, he brings Premier League edge from Sunderland. Dependable when the going gets tough.
players.facts: Geertruida has played in multiple defensive positions for the national team — the Swiss Army knife of the Dutch backline.

players.name: Marten de Roon
players.bio: The veteran engine of Atalanta's midfield, de Roon turns 35 during this tournament — proof that legs can outlast most hairstyles. Combative, dependable, and a serial Serie A overachiever. This World Cup may be his last dance.
players.facts: De Roon helped Atalanta win the UEFA Europa League in 2024 — one of football's great underdog triumphs.

players.name: Virgil van Dijk
players.bio: The colossus. Van Dijk is arguably the world's greatest centre-back — commanding in the air, glacially calm on the ball, and terrifying for strikers. A Liverpool legend nearing his final World Cup at 34.
players.facts: Van Dijk once went nearly two full Premier League seasons without being dribbled past. Strikers simply don't try.

players.name: Nathan Aké
players.bio: Aké is the quietly excellent defender who rarely gets the headlines but always does the job. A Den Haag-born Hague boy who rose through Chelsea's academy to become a Man City and Oranje regular. Cool, smart, reliable.
players.facts: Aké was sold by Chelsea for £190k and bought back for £40 million. Chelsea's loss, everyone's lesson.

players.name: Jan Paul van Hecke
players.bio: The Brondby-born Brighton defender has developed into a technically refined centre-back with an eye for a pass. Van Hecke brings calm and composure — the kind of defender who reads the game before it happens.
players.facts: Van Hecke shares his club with teammate Verbruggen — Brighton have quietly become a Dutch colony on the south coast.

players.name: Justin Kluivert
players.bio: Son of Dutch legend Patrick Kluivert, Justin has carved his own path with pace, trickery and goals from the wing. Bournemouth gave him a home and he repaid them handsomely. The surname helps, but the talent is all his.
players.facts: Justin's father Patrick scored in a Champions League final at age 18. No pressure, lad.

players.name: Ryan Gravenberch
players.bio: The Amsterdam-born midfielder is built like a box-to-box powerhouse but plays with the elegance of a deep-lying playmaker. Gravenberch took Liverpool by storm after a slow start at Bayern. At 24, the best is still ahead.
players.facts: Gravenberch barely played at Bayern Munich but became one of the Premier League's best midfielders within a year at Liverpool.

players.name: Wout Weghorst
players.bio: Weghorst is 6ft 6in of pure determination. The towering Ajax striker won't dazzle you with skill, but he'll outwork everyone on the pitch. Memorably scored twice against Argentina at Qatar 2022 in a stunning comeback.
players.facts: Weghorst's dramatic brace vs Argentina in Qatar 2022 prompted Messi to famously mutter 'What are you looking at, fool?'

players.name: Memphis Depay
players.bio: Depay is the full package off and on the pitch — rapper, tattoo enthusiast, and one of Holland's greatest ever forwards with 55 international goals. Now at Corinthians, still hungry. The lion doesn't change its stripes.
players.facts: Memphis has released his own rap music under the name 'Memphis Depay'. The reviews are… mixed. The goals are not.

players.name: Cody Gakpo
players.bio: Gakpo exploded onto the scene at Qatar 2022 and hasn't looked back. The PSV-to-Liverpool winger is a lethal, left-footed threat with 21 goals in 50 caps. Athletic, direct, and increasingly unplayable.
players.facts: Gakpo scored in each of Netherlands' three group games at the 2022 World Cup — only the second Dutchman ever to do so.

players.name: Mats Wieffer
players.bio: Wieffer is a tough-tackling, high-energy midfielder listed as a defender here but most at home breaking up play in the centre. Brighton's unsung hero, he adds grit to the Oranje's technical game. Don't let the number fool you.
players.facts: Wieffer was playing second-tier Dutch football as recently as 2022 before rapidly rising to the Premier League and international level.

players.name: Robin Roefs
players.bio: Roefs is the third-choice keeper with just one cap to his name — but being in a World Cup squad at 23 is no small feat. The Sunderland stopper is patient, professional, and ready if called upon.
players.facts: Roefs shares his club with two Oranje teammates at Sunderland — the Stadium of Light has gone rather orange.

players.name: Tijjani Reijnders
players.bio: Reijnders made a stunning impact at AC Milan before his big-money move to Man City. The cultured central midfielder has composure beyond his years, with 7 goals from midfield in just 32 caps. A genuine star in the making.
players.facts: Reijnders was a relative unknown before joining AC Milan in 2023 — within one season he was one of Serie A's finest midfielders.

players.name: Micky van de Ven
players.bio: Micky van de Ven is arguably the fastest centre-back on the planet — his speed has been clocked leaving strikers genuinely baffled. The Spurs defender combines raw athleticism with growing composure at 25.
players.facts: Van de Ven's recovery pace is so extreme that Spurs fans began timing his sprints. Scientists have since got involved.

players.name: Guus Til
players.bio: Til is a technically gifted PSV midfielder who adds creativity and a goal threat from deep. With just 7 caps, he's still building his international case — but the Eindhoven stage has given him the perfect showcase.
players.facts: Til won the Eredivisie title with PSV in dominant fashion — the squad was so good it barely felt like a competition.

players.name: Noa Lang
players.bio: Lang is flamboyant, unpredictable, and absolutely loves a trick. The Galatasaray winger has flair to burn and isn't shy about using it. At his best, he's unplayable — at his worst, still very entertaining.
players.facts: Lang is known for his showboating style. Opponents hate it. Neutrals absolutely love it.

players.name: Donyell Malen
players.bio: Malen is lightning quick with sharp movement and a poacher's eye for goal. The Roma forward has 13 international goals and the ability to unlock defences in an instant. Deadly in transition, dangerous everywhere.
players.facts: Malen came through Arsenal's academy before returning to the Netherlands — the Gunners let a future international slip away.

players.name: Brian Brobbey
players.bio: Brobbey is a bulldozing centre-forward with pace and power that unsettles any defence. The Amsterdam-born striker — now at Sunderland — has elite potential and a physical presence that belies his 24 years.
players.facts: Brobbey once played for Ajax's youth rivals before returning to the club's academy. Amsterdam forgave him eventually.

players.name: Teun Koopmeiners
players.bio: Koopmeiners is technically exceptional — a midfielder who scores important goals and dictates tempo with rare intelligence. His big-money move to Juventus confirmed his elite status. 28 caps, 3 goals, and still growing.
players.facts: Koopmeiners scored 15 goals for Atalanta in 2023/24 from midfield — a remarkable tally that triggered a €59m Juventus bid.

players.name: Frenkie de Jong
players.bio: De Jong is one of the most elegant midfielders of his generation — a Barcelona-style deep playmaker who glides past opponents with ease. Despite injury disruptions, his vision and technique remain world-class.
players.facts: Frenkie de Jong agreed to defer his own wages to help Barcelona through financial turmoil. Not all heroes wear capes.

players.name: Denzel Dumfries
players.bio: Dumfries is the human wrecking ball at right wing-back — a relentless, powerful runner who contributed memorably in Euro 2020 and Qatar 2022. Inter Milan's cult hero is a fan favourite for very good reason.
players.facts: Dumfries scored 3 goals at Euro 2020 from right-back, earning him a move to Inter Milan. Not bad for a former goalkeeper.

players.name: Mark Flekken
players.bio: Flekken is a reliable, experienced keeper who won the Bundesliga title with Leverkusen's historic unbeaten side in 2023/24. Technically sound and composed, he's an excellent squad option behind Verbruggen.
players.facts: Flekken was part of Bayer Leverkusen's unbeaten Bundesliga title-winning squad in 2023/24 — 34 games, zero losses.

players.name: Crysencio Summerville
players.bio: Summerville lit up the Premier League with Leeds before his move to West Ham, earning him an Oranje call-up. The pacy winger has electric dribbling ability and a knack for the spectacular. Only 2 caps — for now.
players.facts: Summerville won the PFA Young Player of the Year award in 2023/24 after a stunning season with relegated Leeds United.

players.name: Jorrel Hato
players.bio: At just 20, Hato is the youngest player in the squad and one of European football's most exciting left-back prospects. The Rotterdam-born Chelsea defender reads the game like a veteran and attacks with confidence.
players.facts: Hato became one of the youngest players to represent Ajax's first team — Chelsea spotted him and moved fast. Very fast.

players.name: Quinten Timber
players.bio: The twin brother of Arsenal's Jurrien Timber, Quinten has built his own reputation at Marseille as a dynamic, box-to-box midfielder. Born in Utrecht, he brings energy, versatility, and a famous surname.
players.facts: Quinten and Jurrien Timber are twins who have played together for the Netherlands — double the Timber, double the trouble.
